quick_skim.title: PLRTune: Importance Pre-Sampling and LLM-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Automatic Database Tuning
quick_skim.abstract: Configuration tuning is critical to database performance, yet automatic database tuning remains challenging due to high-dimensional knob spaces, substantial online tuning cost, unreliable textual hints derived from Large Language Models (LLMs) or community documents, and the difficulty of exploiting the remaining optimization room after initialization.   Hence, we propose PLRTune, a staged database tuning system that leverages workload-specific domain knowledge to identify a reduced search space and further optimize within this promising region. First, we develop an importance pre-sampling and reranking strategy to identify the dominant knob subset in a workload-specific manner and derive a compact state representation. Second, we design an execution-guided hint refinement technique to improve the initialization quality of documentation-guided tuning. Finally, we propose a post-tuning refinement stage that leverages Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) to explore the dominant knob subset and further exploit the remaining optimization room.   We evaluate PLRTune on MySQL and PostgreSQL across diverse benchmark workloads. Compared with state-of-the-art approaches, PLRTune achieves the best final result on all tested workloads, improving over the corresponding best-performing alternative by 9.50% on average. Moreover, PLRTune reaches the strongest baseline's best performance level 9.03 times faster on average across workloads, demonstrating its practical runtime efficiency without sacrificing final tuning quality.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM-guided reinforcement learning for automatic database tuning (optimization)
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: LLM引导强化学习进行自动数据库调优（优化）
quick_skim.llm_evidence: LLM引导强化学习进行自动数据库调优（优化）
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: LLM引导强化学习进行自动数据库调优（优化）
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes PLRTune using LLM-guided RL and importance pre-sampling for automatic database configuration tuning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对数据库自动调优中高维旋钮空间和在线调优成本高的问题，提出PLRTune系统。首先通过重要性预采样和重排序识别关键旋钮子集，然后利用LLM引导的强化学习在缩小后的搜索空间中进行优化。实验证明PLRTune在调优效率和最终性能上均显著优于现有方法，将LLM的领域知识用于配置优化。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对数据库自动调优中高维旋钮空间和在线调优成本高的问题，提出PLRTune系统。首先通过重要性预采样和重排序识别关键旋钮子集，然后利用LLM引导的强化学习在缩小后的搜索空间中进行优化。实验证明PLRTune在调优效率和最终性能上均显著优于现有方法，将LLM的领域知识用于配置优化。

quick_skim.title: Bridging Functional Correctness and Runtime Efficiency Gaps in LLM-Based Code Translation
quick_skim.abstract: While large language models (LLMs) have greatly advanced the functional correctness of automated code translation systems, the runtime efficiency of translated programs has received comparatively little attention. With the waning of Moore's law, runtime efficiency has become increasingly important for program quality, alongside functional correctness. Our preliminary study reveals that LLM-translated programs often run slower than human-written ones, and this issue cannot be remedied through prompt engineering alone. Therefore, our work proposes SwiftTrans, a code translation framework comprising two key stages: (1) Multi-Perspective Exploration, where MpTranslator leverages parallel in-context learning (ICL) to generate diverse translation candidates; and (2) Difference-Aware Selection, where DiffSelector identifies the optimal candidate by explicitly comparing differences between translations. We further introduce Hierarchical Guidance for MpTranslator and Ordinal Guidance for DiffSelector, enabling LLMs to better adapt to these two core components. To support the evaluation of runtime efficiency in translated programs, we extend existing benchmarks, CodeNet and F2SBench, and introduce a new benchmark, SwiftBench. Experimental results across all three benchmarks show that SwiftTrans achieves consistent improvements in both correctness and runtime efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM-based code translation focusing on runtime efficiency
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于LLM的代码翻译，关注运行时效率
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于LLM的代码翻译，关注运行时效率
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于LLM的代码翻译，关注运行时效率
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SwiftTrans to improve runtime efficiency of LLM-translated code through multi-perspective exploration and parallel ICL.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文发现LLM翻译的代码在运行时效率上显著低于人类编写代码，且仅靠提示工程无法解决。为此提出SwiftTrans框架，通过多视角探索阶段利用并行上下文学习生成多样翻译，再结合效率评估选择最优版本。实验表明SwiftTrans在保持功能正确性的同时大幅提升翻译代码的运行时效率，弥补了LLM代码翻译在效率维度的空白。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文发现LLM翻译的代码在运行时效率上显著低于人类编写代码，且仅靠提示工程无法解决。为此提出SwiftTrans框架，通过多视角探索阶段利用并行上下文学习生成多样翻译，再结合效率评估选择最优版本。实验表明SwiftTrans在保持功能正确性的同时大幅提升翻译代码的运行时效率，弥补了LLM代码翻译在效率维度的空白。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: code generation for optimization problems using LLM

quick_skim.title: A Solver-Free Training Method for Predict-then-Optimize
quick_skim.abstract: We propose a scalable method for training prediction (machine learning) models in the predict-then-optimize paradigm, where model outputs serve as coefficients for a subsequent linear optimization task. Directly minimizing the empirical decision regret is intractable for linear programming and combinatorial optimization since the decision mapping is piecewise constant, and the gradients are zero almost everywhere. While existing methods address this by smoothing the differentiation process, they suffer from scalability issues, since a computationally expensive solver call is required for every gradient evaluation. To address this, we propose a decision-focused learning pipeline based on a measure transformation principle, which yields a new surrogate loss that is completely optimization-solver-free during training. We establish theoretical guarantees, including Fisher consistency and excess risk bounds. Empirically, our method achieves decision quality competitive with state-of-the-art methods while reducing training time by orders of magnitude.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a solver-free training method for predict-then-optimize, using measure transformation to avoid expensive solver calls.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对预测后优化范式中的可扩展性问题，提出一种基于测度变换的无求解器训练方法。传统方法需要调用求解器计算梯度，而该方法通过构造新代理损失避免求解器调用，显著提升训练效率。实验证明该方法在保持决策质量的同时大幅降低计算开销，为大规模决策聚焦学习提供新路径。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对预测后优化范式中的可扩展性问题，提出一种基于测度变换的无求解器训练方法。传统方法需要调用求解器计算梯度，而该方法通过构造新代理损失避免求解器调用，显著提升训练效率。实验证明该方法在保持决策质量的同时大幅降低计算开销，为大规模决策聚焦学习提供新路径。

quick_skim.title: FAPO: Fully Autonomous Prompt Optimization of Multi-Step LLM Pipelines
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-step LLM pipelines fail through interactions among retrieval, reasoning, and formatting steps, so prompt-only optimization can miss bottlenecks in the chain. We present FAPO (Fully Autonomous Prompt Optimization), a framework that lets Claude Code optimize an LLM pipeline inside a standardized codebase. FAPO evaluates a pipeline, inspects intermediate steps, diagnoses failures, proposes scoped changes, and validates variants repeatedly to optimize against a score function. It first tries prompt edits and, only when prompt optimization appears insufficient, changes chain structure within the permitted scope when attribution identifies a structural bottleneck. Across six benchmarks and three task models, FAPO beats the baseline GEPA in 15 of 18 model-benchmark comparisons. In 11 model-benchmark comparisons, FAPO wins with non-overlapping mean $\pm$ trial-standard-deviation ranges, and the mean FAPO-GEPA gain is +14.1 pp. In the six HoVer and IFBench comparisons where prompt-first search escalated to structural changes, FAPO wins all six with a mean gain of +33.8 pp. FAPO also improves performance on security tasks: on CTIBench-RCM, a security CVE-to-CWE task, prompt-only FAPO lifts test accuracy by +4.0 pp on GPT-5, +7.1 pp on Foundation-Sec-8B-Instruct, and +2.0 pp on Foundation-Sec-8B-Reasoning. These results position FAPO as a state-of-the-art pipeline optimization technique for both general-purpose and security-focused tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: fully autonomous prompt optimization for LLM pipelines, applicable to automatic optimization solution generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 全自主提示优化多步LLM流水线，可用于自动生成优化解决方案
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 全自主提示优化多步LLM流水线，可用于自动生成优化解决方案

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: FAPO autonomously optimizes multi-step LLM pipelines by inspecting intermediate steps and diagnosing failures, applicable to automatic solution generation for optimization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多步LLM流水线常因步骤间交互而失败，仅优化提示无法解决瓶颈。FAPO框架通过评估流水线、检查中间步骤、诊断失败并提出范围变更，自主优化流水线。在多个基准上优于基线，为自动生成优化解决方案提供方法支持。

quick_skim.title: Interpreting Neural Combinatorial Optimization via Evolving Programmatic Bottlenecks
quick_skim.abstract: Neural Combinatorial Optimization (NCO) achieves strong performance, yet its black-box nature remains a key roadblock to deployment and scientific diagnosis. Standard interpretability tools, such as Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs), are ill-equipped for NCO, whose decisions are dynamic, state-dependent, and lack proper concept vocabulary definition. To close this gap, we introduce Evolving Programmatic Bottlenecks (EPB), to our knowledge, the first framework for interpreting NCO policies by distilling black-box NCO models into human-readable program portfolios. EPB employs an LLM to autonomously evolve a bank of programs, where each program's per-step action distribution serves as the bottleneck. EPB works through an iterative framework: Block I fixes program bank capacity and introduces a hybrid textual-numerical gradient descent scheme that couples numerical gradients for student router updates and textual gradients for LLM-based program revision; Block II dynamically adapts bank capacity via fault-targeted expansion and redundancy pruning. Extensive experiments demonstrate EPB's effectiveness and broad applicability, where the distilled program portfolios largely match original performance. EPB also reveals that NCO behavior shifts across optimization stages and can be approximated as a composition of classic heuristic variants. Our work advances interpretable NCO and establishes EPB as a promising tool for interpreting sequential decision-making models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM evolves programmatic bottlenecks for interpreting neural combinatorial optimization
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用LLM演化程序瓶颈来解释神经组合优化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用LLM演化程序瓶颈来解释神经组合优化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用LLM演化程序瓶颈来解释神经组合优化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes EPB, a framework using LLMs to evolve human-readable program portfolios that interpret black-box neural combinatorial optimization policies.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对黑箱神经组合优化（NCO）模型的可解释性问题，提出了一种名为演化程序瓶颈（EPB）的新框架。EPB利用大型语言模型（LLM）自主演化出一组程序，每个程序的每步动作分布作为瓶颈，从而将NCO策略蒸馏成可读的程序组合。实验表明，EPB能够生成可解释且有效的程序，为理解NCO决策提供了一种新途径。该方法将LLM用于优化领域的程序生成，与基于LLM进行优化建模与代码生成的总体主题相关。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对黑箱神经组合优化（NCO）模型的可解释性问题，提出了一种名为演化程序瓶颈（EPB）的新框架。EPB利用大型语言模型（LLM）自主演化出一组程序，每个程序的每步动作分布作为瓶颈，从而将NCO策略蒸馏成可读的程序组合。实验表明，EPB能够生成可解释且有效的程序，为理解NCO决策提供了一种新途径。该方法将LLM用于优化领域的程序生成，与基于LLM进行优化建模与代码生成的总体主题相关。
